campbell.title: Campbell City Council — July 7, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council is holding public hearings on renewing an interim ordinance tied to a state Senate law and on revoking business licenses and tobacco retailer permits for two local shops, California Cigar and Hayes Smoke Shop. The council will also vote on applying for a grant to fund public works projects and on approving new consultant service agreements.
keyTopics: Renewal of interim ordinance implementing a state Senate bill
Revocation of business license and tobacco retailer permit for California Cigar
Revocation of business license and tobacco retailer permit for Hayes Smoke Shop
Public Works Director authorized to submit a grant application
Approval of new consultant service agreements

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — May 6, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council met to approve financial reports and move forward with an ordinance that will be formally adopted later in May. The council also discussed whether the city should become a charter city and reviewed potential tax options and revenue estimates.
keyTopics: Preliminary approval of the Engineer's Report for fiscal year 2026
New ordinance scheduled for final adoption on May 20, 2026
Public hearing conducted on a resolution of intention
Charter city status—council exploring benefits and providing direction to staff
Preliminary tax options and revenue estimates presented for consideration

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos Town Council — April 21, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos Town Council met to handle routine business, including approving prior meeting minutes and reviewing the town's February 2026 financial and investment report. The council also acted on several contracts and agreements, including a construction contract, a professional services agreement, and an amendment to an existing deal, and heard a court-ordered appeal along with public comments.
keyTopics: Approval of April 7, 2026 Closed Session meeting minutes
February 2026 monthly financial and investment report
Construction contract with QLM, Inc. and related improvement resolution
Three-year professional services agreement and a second amendment to an existing contract
Court-ordered appeal hearing and public comments on agenda items

san-jose.title: San José City Council — June 23, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to consider several items, including a historic landmark designation for a South Sixth Street property, a possible ballot measure on ranked-choice voting for filling vacant mayor or council seats, and business deals covering airport advertising and a long-term energy purchase agreement. Residents had the opportunity to comment on these and other agenda items, as well as raise other city business during Open Forum.
keyTopics: Historic landmark and Mills Act contract designation for 647 South Sixth Street
Proposed November 2026 ballot measure allowing ranked-choice voting for filling mayor or council vacancies
Award decision for large-format advertising signage at San José Mineta International Airport
Long-term power purchase agreement with Shell Energy North America
Public comment process and multilingual access options for the meeting

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — June 23, 2026
mountain-view.summary: Mountain View's City Council met partly behind closed doors to discuss hiring a new City Attorney and to consult with lawyers about ongoing lawsuits, including a water pollution case brought by San Francisco Baykeeper against the city and Sunnyvale. The council also reviewed a proposed residential development project at 333 Franklin Street.
keyTopics: Proposed residential development at 333 Franklin Street
Closed-session discussion on appointing a new City Attorney
Legal update on San Francisco Baykeeper v. City of Mountain View and Sunnyvale (federal lawsuit)
Closed-session discussion of anticipated litigation involving city signage rules

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — June 23, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: Sunnyvale's City Council held a closed-door session at 6 PM to discuss confidential matters not detailed in the public agenda, followed by a regular open council meeting at 7 PM that began with a flag salute and time for public comments. No specific policy items or votes were listed in the available agenda details.
keyTopics: Closed session discussion
Regular 7 PM council meeting convened
Flag salute ceremony
Public comment period

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — July 7, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a study session to review updates to the Health and Safety section of the city's long-term General Plan, which covers things like disaster preparedness and safety standards through 2040. The meeting also included celebratory recognitions: honoring the Parks and Recreation Department staff for July's Parks and Recreation Month, and congratulating the Monta Vista High School Science Olympiad Team for their competition achievement.
keyTopics: Review of proposed updates to the Health and Safety Element of the General Plan (2015-2040 Community Vision)
Discussion of disaster preparedness and safety policies affecting future city planning
Recognition of Parks and Recreation Department staff across administration, youth programs, senior services, and sports divisions
Special recognition of Monta Vista High School's Science Olympiad Team for their competitive achievement

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — July 16, 2026
santa-clara.summary: This meeting notice mainly contains a standard legal disclosure about deadlines for challenging city decisions in court, rather than details on specific agenda items. It informs residents that if the city makes a quasi-adjudicative decision (such as certain permits or land-use rulings), any lawsuit contesting that decision must generally be filed within 90 days.
keyTopics: Legal deadline for challenging city decisions in court
90-day filing window under Code of Civil Procedure Section 1094.6
Applies specifically to quasi-adjudicative decisions
Standard procedural disclosure included with meeting agenda

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: Milpitas City Council met to review changes to how federal housing and community development funds are allocated under the city's 2025-2030 Consolidated Plan, along with a batch of new project ideas submitted by individual councilmembers. The council also considered tightening the rules for filling a vacant council seat and handled routine business like approving past meeting minutes and previewing upcoming agenda items.
keyTopics: Proposed amendment to the 2025-2030 Consolidated Plan and 2025-2026 funding plan (affects federal housing/community development grants)
New workplan item requests submitted by individual councilmembers
Proposed ordinance changes to the process for filling a City Council vacancy
Review of the city's upcoming council meeting calendar
Approval of minutes from prior council meetings

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — June 15, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council recognized retiring city staff member Bill Gutoff with a formal proclamation. The bulk of the meeting focused on a required public hearing (Proposition 218) where the Finance Committee recommended council approval of new water, wastewater, and refuse (trash) utility rates for Fiscal Year 2027, along with updated financial forecasts and reserve fund policies for these services.
keyTopics: Proclamation honoring Bill Gutoff's retirement
Proposition 218 public hearing on utility rate increases
Water utility rate changes (schedules W-1 through W-4 and W-7) and reserve fund transfers
Wastewater collection rate changes (schedules S-1, S-2, S-6, S-7) and financial forecast
Refuse/trash collection rate updates (schedules R-1 and R-C)
